industry: Data Center Infrastructure / AI Server Integration
thesis: Data-center services company transformed into an AI infrastructure pure-play. FY2025 revenue of $246M (+66% YoY) and $15.1M net income (turned profitable). Core growth driver is a major multi-year AI rack integration contract with a hyperscaler planning to double AI infrastructure in 2026. Strong balance sheet with $85.5M cash. High customer concentration and lumpy revenue create asymmetric risk/reward.

financials.runway: Strong balance sheet, minimal funding risk

drivers: AI demand surge drives doubling of rack integration volumes in 2026
New AI customer wins reduce concentration risk
Margin expansion as systems integration mix increases
Additional facilities management contracts add recurring revenue
Strategic M&A interest from larger integrators

base_case.assumptions: Revenue grows 25-35% in FY2026 to ~$310-330M. Existing major customer relationship remains stable with moderate new wins. Gross margins stabilize in mid-to-high teens. Stock trades at 1.3-1.6x sales.

risks: Loss or reduction of major AI customer relationship
Revenue lumpiness creates under-absorption and margin compression
New customer wins fail to materialize
AI capex downturn reduces demand for data center buildouts
Competition from larger integrators with greater scale (Flex, Jabil, Celestica)

catalysts.event: Q2 Earnings — validates revenue and margin trajectory
Q3 Earnings — potential FY2026 guidance raise
New Customer Announcements — diversification reduces concentration risk
Major Contract Renewal / Expansion — multi-year revenue visibility
Hyperscaler AI Capex Updates — demand signal for TSSI's core business

key_risks: High customer concentration — one major AI customer represents majority of revenue
Revenue lumpiness from large, unpredictable AI infrastructure orders
Margin compression risk from competitive bidding on procurement contracts
Competition from larger integrators with greater scale (Flex, Jabil, Celestica)
Recent IPO (Nov 2024) with limited public track record and high volatility
AI capex cycle downturn could stall demand for data center buildouts
